story: Quinhagak locals bridge drones and traditional knowledge, now they're teaching others
honesty_guardrail: The KYUK/KNBA/KDLG reporting NEVER uses the words AI, autonomy, autonomous, or computer vision. It frames the win around Yup'ik traditional land knowledge. Any AI/autonomy language is OURS, tied to the drone PLATFORM (Skydio X10) and the state SOAR program (2025 background), never to the KYUK reporting. Keep the human-knowledge frame primary. Reindeer/salmon computer-vision COUNTING is an ASPIRATION (a trainee's stated goal / program direction), not a deployed system, and must be phrased 'wants to' / 'aims to'.

safe_set.label: KYUK/reporting
safe_set.text: Quinhagak, on the Yukon-Kuskokwim Delta, western Alaska

safe_set.label: KYUK/reporting
safe_set.text: Nalaquq, the tech-solutions arm of Quinhagak's village corporation (do NOT name the corporation)

safe_set.label: KYUK/reporting
safe_set.text: Angelina Olrun, 19, a reindeer herder from Mekoryuk, training to fly

safe_set.label: KYUK/reporting
safe_set.text: Byron Petluska, Quinhagak search and rescue, also a fish counter

safe_set.label: KYUK/reporting
safe_set.text: Sean Gleason, drone-training instructor and former archaeologist (Nalaquq R&D lead per Nalaquq)

safe_set.label: KYUK quote (Petluska)
safe_set.text: We get weekly calls for search and rescue, weekly. Sometimes three times a week. Sometimes every day.

safe_set.label: KYUK quote (Petluska)
safe_set.text: It locates people way faster. It keeps the team ready to go straight towards the person they're looking for.

safe_set.label: KYUK quote (Gleason)
safe_set.text: our Yup'ik and Alaska Native pilots tend to excel in those spaces because it's where they grew up

safe_set.label: KYUK quote (Olrun)
safe_set.text: I'm doing this so I could count reindeer, like, basically find reindeer.

safe_set.label: KYUK/reporting
safe_set.text: The drones first came to survey the Nunalleq archaeological dig near Quinhagak

safe_set.label: per Nalaquq (2025 program)
safe_set.text: a 14-week drone-training course; villages Quinhagak, Eek, Goodnews (Bay), Platinum

safe_set.label: per Nalaquq
safe_set.text: Skydio drone training completed with Quinhagak, Eek, and Goodnews Bay

safe_set.label: per 2025 Alaska Business Magazine (state grant)
safe_set.text: $12.4 million federal SMART grant to Alaska DOT&PF for rural drones (SOAR program)

safe_set.label: per 2025 state grant announcement
safe_set.text: US-made Skydio X10; SOAR pursues Beyond Visual Line of Sight, drone-in-a-box, Starlink

safe_set.label: per 2025 reporting
safe_set.text: 22 drones and docking stations already stationed in Alaska, 35 more planned (do NOT say '57 planned statewide')

killed: The parent village-corporation proper name (Qanirtuuq) — unverified, never on screen.
The six-day power outage / drones-inspect-power-lines detail — unverified, excluded.
Specific Skydio X10 autonomy specs (obstacle avoidance / navigation / subject tracking) as reporting — only usable as a labeled Skydio product spec, not as fact from the story.
A deployed reindeer/salmon computer-vision COUNTING layer — unsupported; only as aspiration ('wants to count reindeer').
'57 drones planned statewide' — distorts the source (50 across AK+CA = 35 AK + 15 CA, beyond 22 stationed).
Attributing the 'locates people faster' quote to Gleason — it is Petluska.
